title: parahebe velutina
intro: Parahebe velutina, also known as the hairy spiderflower, is a flowering plant native to New Zealand. It is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant has been traditionally used to treat a variety of health issues, such as digestive problems, skin irritations, and respiratory infections. Herbal preparations of Parahebe velutina include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to relieve symptoms of colds, coughs, and other respiratory ailments.
